title: The Bangladesh Insurance Corporation (Dissolution) Order, 1972 (President's Order)
title_en: The Bangladesh Insurance Corporation (Dissolution) Order, 1972 (President's Order)
description: WHEREAS it is expedient to provide for the dissolution of the Bangladesh Insurance Corporation, and for matters ancillary thereto; NOW, THEREFORE, in pursuance of paragraph 3 of the Fourth Schedule to the Constitution of the People’s Republic of Bangladesh, and in exercise of all powers enabling him in that behalf, the President is pleased to make the following Order:-

provisions.content:
1. (1) This Order may be called the Bangladesh Insurance Corporation (Dissolution) Order, 1972.
(2) It extends to the whole of Bangladesh.
(3) It shall come into force at once.

definitions.definition: the Bangladesh Insurance Corporation established by the Bangladesh Insurance Corporation Order, 1972 (P.O. No. 18 of 1972)
the Teesta Bima Corporation established by the Bangladesh Insurance (Nationalisation) Order, 1972 (P.O. No. 95 of 1972)
the Government of the People's Republic of Bangladesh
an insurer as defined in the Insurance Act, 1938 (Act IV of 1938), who carries on Insurance business in Bangladesh and includes a provident Society as defined in section 65 of that Act
the Pakistan Insurance Corporation established by the Pakistan Insurance Corporation Act, 1952 (Act XXXVIII of 1952)

the Karnaphuli Bima Corporation, the Teesta Bima Corporation, the Surma Jiban Bima Corporation or the Rupsa Jiban Bima Corporation established by Article 4 of the Bangladesh Insurance (Nationalisation) Order, 1972 (P.O. No. 95 of 1972).
